text: <par>Could the second syllable be cognate with “belly” ? Note the similarity of this form to the Monpa Tilang word for “navel” .</par>
<par>The last syll. a Tani suffix? Cf. Idu</par>
<par>Variation in the reflex of <xref ref="363">#363</xref> in BEARD and EYEBROW.</par>
<par>Note the similarity to the Kanauri form for ‘throat’ : <latinform>gr̀dblquote olör̀quote n</latinform> (18130,118664).</par>
<par>Prob. 1319 and not 1320? Add LIVER to 1319 gloss.</par>
<par>BP pfx? 572? ?change 572 to (l)? or change gloss of 228/229?</par>

<par>Bokar Lhoba <latinform>tɕu rə</latinform> a loanword from Tibetan, referring actually to a kind of dried cheese (cf. WT <latinform>ru-ma</latinform> 'curdled milk, used as a ferment' (Jäschke 1881/1958:531)).</par>
<par>The second syllable is a borrowing from Tibetan.</par>
<par>M1: or see <emph>ka(t)</emph>? or <emph>ge-ma</emph>? M2: s or 200?</par>
<par>M2: s or e.g. 1384 (or even 1392 w/added gloss)?</par>
<par>M1: 650? rel. to 1390? M2: 191? M3: or 229? (if expand gloss) M4: 658?</par>

<par>Note the similarity between the Monpa forms for 'tail' and the Mru forms <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote ɛmyi³</latinform> (163260) and <latinform>ʃr̀dblquote imi³</latinform> (163261); the 'p' in the Monpa forms may be epenthetic.</par>
<par>or 1381? But cf. below 117409 and 144322 (Jirel): 1 morpheme only? and above *TB 144329</par>
<par>Tsangla (Motuo) <latinform>tɕʰu ra</latinform> is a loanword from Tibetan, referring to a kind of dried cheese (cf. Bokar Lhoba <latinform>tɕu rə</latinform>).</par>
<par>The final nasal in this form is unexplained, but is perhaps due to assimilation to a now-lost suffix with nasal initial (cf. Milang <latinform>ɟim-ma</latinform>).</par>
